sections.about: This metric measures the share of turnover from products/services that carry information on recycling and/or safe disposal. It is a BRSR Principle 9 Essential Indicator because it relates to consumer information on reuse, recycling, and safe end-of-life handling. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: JSW reported full 100% coverage in all three years, while SAIL reported 100% in FY2023 and FY2024 before dropping to 0% in FY2025. Jindal Stainless improved sharply from 4.1% in FY2023 to 100% in FY2024 and FY2025, supported by stainless steel’s circularity proposition: JSL states that end-of-life stainless steel scrap is not treated as waste but as a valuable input for remelting, while safe disposal is managed through authorised recyclers. Tata Steel reported 19% in FY2023, 16% in FY2024, and 17% in FY2025, despite strong underlying circularity actions: in FY2024-25 Tata recycled about 4.2 million tonnes of scrap, including approximately 1.7 million tonnes internal and 2.5 million tonnes external scrap.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 3rd at 17%, behind JSW and Jindal at 100%; the gap to the best performer is 83 percentage points, or 83% below the 100% benchmark. Tata’s trajectory is mixed: it worsened from 19% to 16% in FY2024, then improved marginally to 17% in FY2025, but did not close the structural gap to full-coverage reporters. This is an important positioning issue because Tata has substantive circular-economy capability through scrap use, IBMD by-product valorisation, and Steel Recycling Business, but the consumer-facing turnover coverage on recycling/safe-disposal information remains low relative to peers.
sections.targets: Related targets include Tata Steel Nederland’s aim to increase scrap usage from 17% in 2019 to 30% by 2030 and Tata Steel UK’s EAF transition, expected to increase scrap usage from 17% to over 70%; Jindal Stainless is working toward Zero-Waste-to-Landfill certification by 2030 and promotes a closed-loop recycling system.
sections.comparabilityNotes: This indicator measures information coverage as a percentage of turnover, not actual recycling rate, scrap charge, by-product utilisation, or waste diversion. Tata’s low value therefore should not be read as low circularity performance; it reflects that only 17% of FY2024-25 turnover carried the relevant recycling/safe-disposal information.
